mission.title: atlantis: Changes in recloning for merge strategy leads to disappearing plans
mission.description: Changes in recloning for merge strategy leads to disappearing plans. This issue affects 30+ users.

steps.title: Identify atlantis troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your atlantis deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis -n atlantis --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review atlantis configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant atlantis configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl get configmap -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=atlantis
```
### Community Note

* Please vote on this issue by adding a 👍 [reaction](https://blog.github.com/2016-03-10-add-reactions-to-pull-requests-issues-and-comments/) to the original issue to help the community and maintainers prioritize this request.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Changes in recloning for merge strategy leads to…
steps.description:
## what

When using the merge checkout strategy, only check for upstream changes and re-clone when creating a plan. 

## why

Previously we would check for upstream changes and merge them during any call to Clone(), but if we had already made a plan, this would remove the generated plan. This would
```yaml
Top of my repos.yaml:
```

steps.title: Confirm Changes in recloning for merge strategy leads to… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis -n atlantis --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n atlantis --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
## what

When using the merge checkout strategy, only check for upstream changes and re-clone when creating a plan. 

## why

Previously we would check for upstream changes and merge them during any call to Clone(), but if we had already made a plan, this would remove the generated plan.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with atlantis installed or the issue environment reproducible.
